name: PJC — Premature Junctional Contractions
identifyBy: Early QRS with no preceding P wave OR retrograde (inverted) P wave in II/III/aVF
Usually followed by resumption of normal sinus rhythm
Less common than PACs — often asymptomatic

measurements.pWaves: Absent or retrograde (inverted II/III/aVF) in ectopic beat
measurements.prInterval: Short or none for ectopic beat

management: Isolated PJCs: benign — no treatment needed
Address triggers: digitalis toxicity is a classic cause
Frequent PJCs: monitor, check digoxin levels if applicable
Consider echocardiogram to exclude structural disease
pearls: PJCs are much less common than PACs — when frequent, check for digoxin toxicity
Differentiation from PACs: no P wave or retrograde P in PJCs; abnormal P morphology in PACs
Non-compensatory pause distinguishes ectopics from complete heart block
PJCs in narrow complex bigeminy: AV node has enhanced automaticity — classic with digoxin
